name: ddl-edge-cases
description: DDL edge cases — unusual table definitions, types, and constraints.

cases.name: empty_table_scan
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE Empty(id INT64, PRIMARY KEY(id))

cases.name: all_supported_types
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE TypeTest(id INT64, b BOOLEAN, s STRING, d DOUBLE, PRIMARY KEY(id))
cases.query: MATCH (t:TypeTest) RETURN t.id, t.b, t.s, t.d

cases.name: string_with_special_chars
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE S(val STRING, PRIMARY KEY(val))

cases.name: boolean_filter
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE Flag(id INT64, active BOOLEAN, PRIMARY KEY(id))
cases.query: MATCH (f:Flag) WHERE f.active = true RETURN f.id ORDER BY f.id

cases.name: negative_integers
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE Val(id INT64, v INT64, PRIMARY KEY(id))

cases.name: double_precision
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE D(id INT64, val DOUBLE, PRIMARY KEY(id))

cases.name: multiple_node_tables
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE City(name STRING, PRIMARY KEY(name))
CREATE NODE TABLE Country(name STRING, PRIMARY KEY(name))

cases.name: rel_with_properties
setup: CREATE NODE TABLE Person(name STRING, PRIMARY KEY(name))
CREATE REL TABLE Follows(FROM Person TO Person, since INT64)
